revision.comment: Created by translating the page "[[:ja:Special:Redirect/revision/82108941|生祀]]"
revision.text:
Seishi is to enshrine the spirit of a living person. It is also called a living shrine. There are two types of enshrinement: enshrining the spirit of a living person who has made a significant contribution, and enshrining one's own spirit. [[File:Hiraga_Gennai_seishi01s2040.jpg|thumb|200x200px|[[Hiraga Gennai|平賀源内]]生祠（[[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]][[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|福山市]]鞆町）]]
== 概略 ==
=== 中国における生祠 === [[Han Dynasty#Western Han|前漢]]の欒布が[[Yan Kingdom (Han dynasty)|燕]]の丞相であった時、燕と[[Qi (state)|斉]]の間にその社を立てて、「欒公社」と呼んだ。また[[:zh:石慶]]が斉の丞相であった時、斉人は「石相祠」を建てた。This is the beginning of the birth shrine.<ref>[[Zhao Yi]], "The New New Ancestral Hall", Volume 32, "Ancestral Hall", "This is the beginning of the establishment of an ancestral hall.</ref>
[[Tang dynasty|唐]]代では、現任の官員が碑や祠を建てることに一定の制限があった。『[[Tang Code|唐律疏義]]』によればみだりに生祠や德政碑を建てたものは、「諸在官長吏實無政跡輒立碑者、徒一年（官吏で功績がないのに石碑を建てたものは、徒罪一年）」と徒罪一年の処分を受けなければならなかった。<ref>The [[Tang Code]], Volume 11 (The Law of Office)</ref>。The Tang Dynasty called it "the difficulty of building monuments and shrines on the first day.
[[Ming dynasty|明]]代では、[[Tengzhou|滕州市]]の百姓が、当地で清廉な政治を行い燕京に赴任することになった[[:zh:趙邦清]]を記念して、生祠を作ったところ、「黃童白叟、羅而拜之（子供から年寄りまで、列をなして拝んだ）」という。
明の[[Tianqi Emperor|天啓帝]]の時代、宦官の[[:zh:司禮監]][[:zh:秉筆太監]]であった[[Wei Zhongxian|魏忠賢]]は自ら政治を行って天下を支配し、媚びへつらうものや権勢を畏れるものは彼のために生祠を立てた。天啓七年（1627年）五月、国子監生の陸万齢は、「孔子は『春秋』を作り、忠賢は『要典』を作った。孔子は少正卯を誅し、忠賢は東林を誅した」ため魏忠賢は孔子と並び論じられるべきであると上書した。天啓七年四月、[[Yuan Chonghuan|袁崇煥]]と[[Ministry of War (imperial China)|兵部尚書]]の[[:zh:閻鳴泰]]は上奏して、魏忠賢の功德を讃え、前屯の両地に魏忠賢のため生祠を立てることを求めた<ref>『明熹宗実録』："天啓七年四月“薊遼總督閻鳴泰、巡撫袁崇煥疏，頌魏忠賢功德，請於寧前建祠，賜名懋德。」袁崇煥疏稱:「廠臣魏忠賢功在社稷，海內之共見共聞，業已銘刻金石，無容職贅，至其身任遼事，誓□恢復，梟滅逆虜，任用劉應乾、陶文、紀用等，而關內外禦敵之伏甲軍器馬匹懸簾等項，俱以家資置辦，日逐解來，又助軍需。臣方一意巡緝，嚴警諸營將吏，不敢貪懦營私，不敢餽遺隱串，改虛為實，化賈為真，易怯為勇，以有今日。浞古內臣誰有出其右者！"（『明熹宗七年都察院実録』天啓六年十月）(Is this Chinese? It is from Japanese Wikipedia so I am confused as to translation)</ref>。After that, Wei Zhongxian's raw shrine was "all over the world", "the cost of each shrine was at most hundreds of thousands, at least tens of thousands", "stolen the people's fortune, invaded the public safe, and was cut down. There are countless trees. " When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|顧炎武]]が嘆いていうには「今の代には生祠を建てない官はいない。そして任を去ってから幾年も経たずにその像を壊し、その主人を替える。」という<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== 日本における生祠 === 自己の霊魂を祀る生祀は、長命を得るため、あるいは死後に神となるために行われた。[[Ōkuninushi|大国主命]]が自らの[[Mitama|奇魂]]・[[Mitama|幸魂]]を[[Mount Miwa|三諸山]]（[[Mount Miwa|三輪山]]）に祀った故事に由来するとされる。
自己の霊魂を祀った生祀の文献上で最も古い事例は、[[Heian period|平安時代]]の[[923|923年]]、[[Ise Grand Shrine|伊勢神宮]]の外宮の[[Shinkan (official)|神官]]であった松木春彦（[[824|824年]] - [[924|924年]]）が、伊勢[[Watarai District, Mie|度会郡]]尾部で、石に自己の霊魂を鎮め、祀ったことである。[[Edo period|江戸時代]]、[[Matsudaira Sadanobu|松平定信]]が[[1797|1797年]]、奥州白河城に自分の生祀を成立した例がある。生祀は江戸時代に増えたが、それは中国思想の影響であろうという。[[Edo period|江戸時代]]に、[[Yamazaki Ansai|山崎闇斎]]が[[Confucianism|儒教]]の礼式を参考に[[Festival|祭式]]を考案し、自らの霊魂を祀った。その生祀は1671年、京都の自邸の垂加霊社に成立したものである。これ以後も、[[Shinto|神道]]家や平田派の[[Kokugaku|国学者]]によって、それぞれ独自の祭式で自己の霊魂を祀った。
生祀の研究は加藤玄智（[[1873|1873年]] - [[1965|1965年]]）が有名である。加藤によれば判明している生祀は670あり、新しい生祀は[[1931|1931年]]8月、北海道日高国浦河町に祀られた西忠義(西義一の父。)のものである。
[[Emperor Meiji|明治天皇]]の生祀は[[1876|1876年]]（[[Meiji (era)|明治]]9年）宮城県石巻港に、[[1893|1893年]]（明治26年）[[Kamiina District, Nagano|上伊那郡]]小野村の矢彦神社境内に、建立された。明治天皇、[[Empress Shōken|昭憲皇太后]]の生祀は[[1887|1887年]]（明治20年）および[[1904|1904年]]（明治37年）に2箇所に、[[Emperor Taishō|大正天皇]]の生祀は[[1911|1911年]]（明治44年）[[Hokkaido|北海道]][[Hidaka|日高]]国門別村に、[[Empress Teimei|貞明皇后]]の生祀は[[1913|1913年]]（[[Taishō|大正]]2年）広島県広瀬町に、[[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|秩父宮雍仁親王]]、[[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu|高松宮宣仁親王]]の生祀は[[1924|1924年]]（大正13年）[[Himeji|姫路市]]同心町に成立した。
[[Hirohito|昭和天皇]]の生祀は[[1923|1923年]]（大正12年）北海道[[Tokachi|十勝]]国本別村義経山上に、昭和天皇、[[Empress Kōjun|香淳皇后]]の生祀は[[1928|1928年]]（昭和3年）広島市広瀬町に成立した。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
== 関連項目 ==
* [[Tamaya|祖霊舎]] * 奥都城 [[Category:Shinto]]

revision.comment: /* 中国における生祠 */
revision.text:
Seishi is to enshrine the spirit of a living person. It is also called a living shrine. There are two types of enshrinement: enshrining the spirit of a living person who has made a significant contribution, and enshrining one's own spirit. [[File:Hiraga_Gennai_seishi01s2040.jpg|thumb|200x200px|[[Hiraga Gennai|平賀源内]]生祠（[[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]][[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|福山市]]鞆町）]]
== 概略 ==
=== Sheng shrines in China === When [[Han Dynasty#Western Han|Former Han]]'s Reunobu was the prime minister of [[Yan Kingdom (Han dynasty)|Yan]], he built his shrine between Yan and [[Qi (state)|Qi]] and called it the "Reun Gong Shrine". Also, when [[:zh:Shiqing]] was the prime minister of Qi, the Qi people built the "Shiqing Shrine". This is the beginning of the birth shrine.<ref>[[Zhao Yi]], "The New New Ancestral Hall", Volume 32, "Ancestral Hall", "This is the beginning of the establishment of an ancestral hall.</ref>
In the [[Tang dynasty]], there were certain restrictions on the construction of monuments and shrines by officials in office. According to the [[Tang Code|Tang Rulings and Ordinances]], those who erected shrines or monuments without permission were subject to a penalty of one year of imprisonment.<ref>The [[Tang Code]], Volume 11 (The Law of Office)</ref>。The Tang Dynasty called it "the difficulty of building monuments and shrines on the first day.
[[Ming dynasty|明]]代では、[[Tengzhou|滕州市]]の百姓が、当地で清廉な政治を行い燕京に赴任することになった[[:zh:趙邦清]]を記念して、生祠を作ったところ、「黃童白叟、羅而拜之（子供から年寄りまで、列をなして拝んだ）」という。

It is from Japanese Wikipedia so I am confused as to translation)</ref>。After that, Wei Zhongxian's raw shrine was "all over the world", "the cost of each shrine was at most hundreds of thousands, at least tens of thousands", "stolen the people's fortune, invaded the public safe, and was cut down. There are countless trees. " When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|顧炎武]]が嘆いていうには「今の代には生祠を建てない官はいない。そして任を去ってから幾年も経たずにその像を壊し、その主人を替える。」という<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== 日本における生祠 === 自己の霊魂を祀る生祀は、長命を得るため、あるいは死後に神となるために行われた。[[Ōkuninushi|大国主命]]が自らの[[Mitama|奇魂]]・[[Mitama|幸魂]]を[[Mount Miwa|三諸山]]（[[Mount Miwa|三輪山]]）に祀った故事に由来するとされる。

There are two types of enshrinement: enshrining the spirit of a living person who has made a significant contribution, and enshrining one's own spirit. [[File:Hiraga_Gennai_seishi01s2040.jpg|thumb|200x200px|[[Hiraga Gennai|平賀源内]]生祠（[[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]][[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|福山市]]鞆町）]]

In the [[Ming dynasty|Ming]], peasants in [[Tengzhou|Tengzhou]] built a shrine to commemorate [[:zh:Zhao Bangqing]], who was appointed to Yanjing for his clean politics. The shrine was built to commemorate him.

自己の霊魂を祀った生祀の文献上で最も古い事例は、[[Heian period|平安時代]]の[[923|923年]]、[[Ise Grand Shrine|伊勢神宮]]の外宮の[[Shinkan (official)|神官]]であった松木春彦（[[824|824年]] - [[924|924年]]）が、伊勢[[Watarai District, Mie|度会郡]]尾部で、石に自己の霊魂を鎮め、祀ったことである。[[Edo period|江戸時代]]、[[Matsudaira Sadanobu|松平定信]]が[[1797|1797年]]、奥州白河城に自分の生祀を成立した例がある。生祀は江戸時代に増えたが、それは中国思想の影響であろうという。[[Edo period|江戸時代]]に、[[Yamazaki Ansai|山崎闇斎]]が[[Confucianism|儒教]]の礼式を参考に[[Festival|祭式]]を考案し、自らの霊魂を祀った。その生祀は1671年、京都の自邸の垂加霊社に成立したものである。これ以後も、[[Shinto|神道]]家や平田派の[[Kokugaku|国学者]]によって、それぞれ独自の祭式で自己の霊魂を祀った。
生祀の研究は加藤玄智（[[1873|1873年]] - [[1965|1965年]]）が有名である。加藤によれば判明している生祀は670あり、新しい生祀は[[1931|1931年]]8月、北海道日高国浦河町に祀られた西忠義(西義一の父。)のものである。
[[Emperor Meiji|明治天皇]]の生祀は[[1876|1876年]]（[[Meiji (era)|明治]]9年）宮城県石巻港に、[[1893|1893年]]（明治26年）[[Kamiina District, Nagano|上伊那郡]]小野村の矢彦神社境内に、建立された。明治天皇、[[Empress Shōken|昭憲皇太后]]の生祀は[[1887|1887年]]（明治20年）および[[1904|1904年]]（明治37年）に2箇所に、[[Emperor Taishō|大正天皇]]の生祀は[[1911|1911年]]（明治44年）[[Hokkaido|北海道]][[Hidaka|日高]]国門別村に、[[Empress Teimei|貞明皇后]]の生祀は[[1913|1913年]]（[[Taishō|大正]]2年）広島県広瀬町に、[[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|秩父宮雍仁親王]]、[[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu|高松宮宣仁親王]]の生祀は[[1924|1924年]]（大正13年）[[Himeji|姫路市]]同心町に成立した。
[[Hirohito|昭和天皇]]の生祀は[[1923|1923年]]（大正12年）北海道[[Tokachi|十勝]]国本別村義経山上に、昭和天皇、[[Empress Kōjun|香淳皇后]]の生祀は[[1928|1928年]]（昭和3年）広島市広瀬町に成立した。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />

revision.comment: /* Sheng shrines in China */
revision.text:
Seishi is to enshrine the spirit of a living person. It is also called a living shrine. There are two types of enshrinement: enshrining the spirit of a living person who has made a significant contribution, and enshrining one's own spirit. [[File:Hiraga_Gennai_seishi01s2040.jpg|thumb|200x200px|[[Hiraga Gennai|平賀源内]]生祠（[[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]][[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|福山市]]鞆町）]]
== 概略 ==
=== Sheng shrines in China === When [[Han Dynasty#Western Han|Former Han]]'s Reunobu was the prime minister of [[Yan Kingdom (Han dynasty)|Yan]], he built his shrine between Yan and [[Qi (state)|Qi]] and called it the "Reun Gong Shrine". Also, when [[:zh:Shiqing]] was the prime minister of Qi, the Qi people built the "Shiqing Shrine". This is the beginning of the birth shrine.<ref>[[Zhao Yi]], "The New New Ancestral Hall", Volume 32, "Ancestral Hall", "This is the beginning of the establishment of an ancestral hall.</ref>
In the [[Tang dynasty]], there were certain restrictions on the construction of monuments and shrines by officials in office. According to the [[Tang Code|Tang Rulings and Ordinances]], those who erected shrines or monuments without permission were subject to a penalty of one year of imprisonment.<ref>The [[Tang Code]], Volume 11 (The Law of Office)</ref>。The Tang Dynasty called it "the difficulty of building monuments and shrines on the first day.
In the [[Ming dynasty|Ming]], peasants in [[Tengzhou|Tengzhou]] built a shrine to commemorate [[:zh:Zhao Bangqing]], who was appointed to Yanjing for his clean politics. The shrine was built to commemorate him.
During the reign of the [[Tianqi Emperor]] of the Ming Dynasty, [[Wei Zhongxian]], the eunuch [[:zh:存禮監]] [[:zh:秉筆太監]], ruled the world by conducting his own politics, and those who flattered him or feared his authority were given a He made a living shrine for him. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing (1627), Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote: "Confucius wrote the Spring and Autumn, and Chung-Hsien wrote the Essentials. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote that Confucius had written the "Spring and Autumn" and Chung-Hsien had written the "Essentials. In April of the 7th year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, [[Yuan Chonghuan]] and [[:zh:Yan Mingtai]] of the [[Ministry of War (imperial China)|Shohshoh of the Ministry of War]] made an appeal to the government to praise the merits and demerits of Wei Zhongxian and to establish a living shrine for him in both Qiantu.<ref>『明熹宗実録』："天啓七年四月“薊遼總督閻鳴泰、巡撫袁崇煥疏，頌魏忠賢功德，請於寧前建祠，賜名懋德。」袁崇煥疏稱:「廠臣魏忠賢功在社稷，海內之共見共聞，業已銘刻金石，無容職贅，至其身任遼事，誓□恢復，梟滅逆虜，任用劉應乾、陶文、紀用等，而關內外禦敵之伏甲軍器馬匹懸簾等項，俱以家資置辦，日逐解來，又助軍需。臣方一意巡緝，嚴警諸營將吏，不敢貪懦營私，不敢餽遺隱串，改虛為實，化賈為真，易怯為勇，以有今日。浞古內臣誰有出其右者！"（『明熹宗七年都察院実録』天啓六年十月）(Is this Chinese? It is from Japanese Wikipedia so I am confused as to translation)</ref>。After that, Wei Zhongxian's raw shrine was "all over the world", "the cost of each shrine was at most hundreds of thousands, at least tens of thousands", "stolen the people's fortune, invaded the public safe, and was cut down. There are countless trees. " When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|顧炎武]]が嘆いていうには「今の代には生祠を建てない官はいない。そして任を去ってから幾年も経たずにその像を壊し、その主人を替える。」という<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== 日本における生祠 === 自己の霊魂を祀る生祀は、長命を得るため、あるいは死後に神となるために行われた。[[Ōkuninushi|大国主命]]が自らの[[Mitama|奇魂]]・[[Mitama|幸魂]]を[[Mount Miwa|三諸山]]（[[Mount Miwa|三輪山]]）に祀った故事に由来するとされる。
自己の霊魂を祀った生祀の文献上で最も古い事例は、[[Heian period|平安時代]]の[[923|923年]]、[[Ise Grand Shrine|伊勢神宮]]の外宮の[[Shinkan (official)|神官]]であった松木春彦（[[824|824年]] - [[924|924年]]）が、伊勢[[Watarai District, Mie|度会郡]]尾部で、石に自己の霊魂を鎮め、祀ったことである。[[Edo period|江戸時代]]、[[Matsudaira Sadanobu|松平定信]]が[[1797|1797年]]、奥州白河城に自分の生祀を成立した例がある。生祀は江戸時代に増えたが、それは中国思想の影響であろうという。[[Edo period|江戸時代]]に、[[Yamazaki Ansai|山崎闇斎]]が[[Confucianism|儒教]]の礼式を参考に[[Festival|祭式]]を考案し、自らの霊魂を祀った。その生祀は1671年、京都の自邸の垂加霊社に成立したものである。これ以後も、[[Shinto|神道]]家や平田派の[[Kokugaku|国学者]]によって、それぞれ独自の祭式で自己の霊魂を祀った。
生祀の研究は加藤玄智（[[1873|1873年]] - [[1965|1965年]]）が有名である。加藤によれば判明している生祀は670あり、新しい生祀は[[1931|1931年]]8月、北海道日高国浦河町に祀られた西忠義(西義一の父。)のものである。
[[Emperor Meiji|明治天皇]]の生祀は[[1876|1876年]]（[[Meiji (era)|明治]]9年）宮城県石巻港に、[[1893|1893年]]（明治26年）[[Kamiina District, Nagano|上伊那郡]]小野村の矢彦神社境内に、建立された。明治天皇、[[Empress Shōken|昭憲皇太后]]の生祀は[[1887|1887年]]（明治20年）および[[1904|1904年]]（明治37年）に2箇所に、[[Emperor Taishō|大正天皇]]の生祀は[[1911|1911年]]（明治44年）[[Hokkaido|北海道]][[Hidaka|日高]]国門別村に、[[Empress Teimei|貞明皇后]]の生祀は[[1913|1913年]]（[[Taishō|大正]]2年）広島県広瀬町に、[[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|秩父宮雍仁親王]]、[[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu|高松宮宣仁親王]]の生祀は[[1924|1924年]]（大正13年）[[Himeji|姫路市]]同心町に成立した。
[[Hirohito|昭和天皇]]の生祀は[[1923|1923年]]（大正12年）北海道[[Tokachi|十勝]]国本別村義経山上に、昭和天皇、[[Empress Kōjun|香淳皇后]]の生祀は[[1928|1928年]]（昭和3年）広島市広瀬町に成立した。

There are countless trees. " When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|Gu Yanwu]] lamented, "In this generation, there is no official who does not build a living shrine. And within a few years of leaving office, he would destroy the statues and change their masters. He said.<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。

[[Gu Yanwu|Gu Yanwu]] lamented, "In this generation, there is no official who does not build a living shrine. And within a few years of leaving office, he would destroy the statues and change their masters. He said.<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== 日本における生祠 === 自己の霊魂を祀る生祀は、長命を得るため、あるいは死後に神となるために行われた。[[Ōkuninushi|大国主命]]が自らの[[Mitama|奇魂]]・[[Mitama|幸魂]]を[[Mount Miwa|三諸山]]（[[Mount Miwa|三輪山]]）に祀った故事に由来するとされる。
自己の霊魂を祀った生祀の文献上で最も古い事例は、[[Heian period|平安時代]]の[[923|923年]]、[[Ise Grand Shrine|伊勢神宮]]の外宮の[[Shinkan (official)|神官]]であった松木春彦（[[824|824年]] - [[924|924年]]）が、伊勢[[Watarai District, Mie|度会郡]]尾部で、石に自己の霊魂を鎮め、祀ったことである。[[Edo period|江戸時代]]、[[Matsudaira Sadanobu|松平定信]]が[[1797|1797年]]、奥州白河城に自分の生祀を成立した例がある。生祀は江戸時代に増えたが、それは中国思想の影響であろうという。[[Edo period|江戸時代]]に、[[Yamazaki Ansai|山崎闇斎]]が[[Confucianism|儒教]]の礼式を参考に[[Festival|祭式]]を考案し、自らの霊魂を祀った。その生祀は1671年、京都の自邸の垂加霊社に成立したものである。これ以後も、[[Shinto|神道]]家や平田派の[[Kokugaku|国学者]]によって、それぞれ独自の祭式で自己の霊魂を祀った。
生祀の研究は加藤玄智（[[1873|1873年]] - [[1965|1965年]]）が有名である。加藤によれば判明している生祀は670あり、新しい生祀は[[1931|1931年]]8月、北海道日高国浦河町に祀られた西忠義(西義一の父。)のものである。
[[Emperor Meiji|明治天皇]]の生祀は[[1876|1876年]]（[[Meiji (era)|明治]]9年）宮城県石巻港に、[[1893|1893年]]（明治26年）[[Kamiina District, Nagano|上伊那郡]]小野村の矢彦神社境内に、建立された。明治天皇、[[Empress Shōken|昭憲皇太后]]の生祀は[[1887|1887年]]（明治20年）および[[1904|1904年]]（明治37年）に2箇所に、[[Emperor Taishō|大正天皇]]の生祀は[[1911|1911年]]（明治44年）[[Hokkaido|北海道]][[Hidaka|日高]]国門別村に、[[Empress Teimei|貞明皇后]]の生祀は[[1913|1913年]]（[[Taishō|大正]]2年）広島県広瀬町に、[[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|秩父宮雍仁親王]]、[[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu|高松宮宣仁親王]]の生祀は[[1924|1924年]]（大正13年）[[Himeji|姫路市]]同心町に成立した。
[[Hirohito|昭和天皇]]の生祀は[[1923|1923年]]（大正12年）北海道[[Tokachi|十勝]]国本別村義経山上に、昭和天皇、[[Empress Kōjun|香淳皇后]]の生祀は[[1928|1928年]]（昭和3年）広島市広瀬町に成立した。
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />

revision.comment: /* 関連項目 */
revision.text:
Seishi is to enshrine the spirit of a living person. It is also called a living shrine. There are two types of enshrinement: enshrining the spirit of a living person who has made a significant contribution, and enshrining one's own spirit. [[File:Hiraga_Gennai_seishi01s2040.jpg|thumb|200x200px|[[Hiraga Gennai|平賀源内]]生祠（[[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]][[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|福山市]]鞆町）]]
== Overview ==
=== Sheng shrines in China === When [[Han Dynasty#Western Han|Former Han]]'s Reunobu was the prime minister of [[Yan Kingdom (Han dynasty)|Yan]], he built his shrine between Yan and [[Qi (state)|Qi]] and called it the "Reun Gong Shrine". Also, when [[:zh:Shiqing]] was the prime minister of Qi, the Qi people built the "Shiqing Shrine". This is the beginning of the birth shrine.<ref>[[Zhao Yi]], "The New New Ancestral Hall", Volume 32, "Ancestral Hall", "This is the beginning of the establishment of an ancestral hall.</ref>
In the [[Tang dynasty]], there were certain restrictions on the construction of monuments and shrines by officials in office. According to the [[Tang Code|Tang Rulings and Ordinances]], those who erected shrines or monuments without permission were subject to a penalty of one year of imprisonment.<ref>The [[Tang Code]], Volume 11 (The Law of Office)</ref>。The Tang Dynasty called it "the difficulty of building monuments and shrines on the first day.
In the [[Ming dynasty|Ming]], peasants in [[Tengzhou|Tengzhou]] built a shrine to commemorate [[:zh:Zhao Bangqing]], who was appointed to Yanjing for his clean politics. The shrine was built to commemorate him.
During the reign of the [[Tianqi Emperor]] of the Ming Dynasty, [[Wei Zhongxian]], the eunuch [[:zh:存禮監]] [[:zh:秉筆太監]], ruled the world by conducting his own politics, and those who flattered him or feared his authority were given a He made a living shrine for him. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing (1627), Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote: "Confucius wrote the Spring and Autumn, and Chung-Hsien wrote the Essentials. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote that Confucius had written the "Spring and Autumn" and Chung-Hsien had written the "Essentials. In April of the 7th year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, [[Yuan Chonghuan]] and [[:zh:Yan Mingtai]] of the [[Ministry of War (imperial China)|Shohshoh of the Ministry of War]] made an appeal to the government to praise the merits and demerits of Wei Zhongxian and to establish a living shrine for him in both Qiantu.<ref>『明熹宗実録』："天啓七年四月“薊遼總督閻鳴泰、巡撫袁崇煥疏，頌魏忠賢功德，請於寧前建祠，賜名懋德。」袁崇煥疏稱:「廠臣魏忠賢功在社稷，海內之共見共聞，業已銘刻金石，無容職贅，至其身任遼事，誓□恢復，梟滅逆虜，任用劉應乾、陶文、紀用等，而關內外禦敵之伏甲軍器馬匹懸簾等項，俱以家資置辦，日逐解來，又助軍需。臣方一意巡緝，嚴警諸營將吏，不敢貪懦營私，不敢餽遺隱串，改虛為實，化賈為真，易怯為勇，以有今日。浞古內臣誰有出其右者！"（『明熹宗七年都察院実録』天啓六年十月）(Is this Chinese? It is from Japanese Wikipedia so I am confused as to translation)</ref>。After that, Wei Zhongxian's raw shrine was "all over the world", "the cost of each shrine was at most hundreds of thousands, at least tens of thousands", "stolen the people's fortune, invaded the public safe, and was cut down. There are countless trees. " When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|Gu Yanwu]] lamented, "In this generation, there is no official who does not build a living shrine. And within a few years of leaving office, he would destroy the statues and change their masters. He said.<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== 日本における生祠 === 自己の霊魂を祀る生祀は、長命を得るため、あるいは死後に神となるために行われた。[[Ōkuninushi|大国主命]]が自らの[[Mitama|奇魂]]・[[Mitama|幸魂]]を[[Mount Miwa|三諸山]]（[[Mount Miwa|三輪山]]）に祀った故事に由来するとされる。
自己の霊魂を祀った生祀の文献上で最も古い事例は、[[Heian period|平安時代]]の[[923|923年]]、[[Ise Grand Shrine|伊勢神宮]]の外宮の[[Shinkan (official)|神官]]であった松木春彦（[[824|824年]] - [[924|924年]]）が、伊勢[[Watarai District, Mie|度会郡]]尾部で、石に自己の霊魂を鎮め、祀ったことである。[[Edo period|江戸時代]]、[[Matsudaira Sadanobu|松平定信]]が[[1797|1797年]]、奥州白河城に自分の生祀を成立した例がある。生祀は江戸時代に増えたが、それは中国思想の影響であろうという。[[Edo period|江戸時代]]に、[[Yamazaki Ansai|山崎闇斎]]が[[Confucianism|儒教]]の礼式を参考に[[Festival|祭式]]を考案し、自らの霊魂を祀った。その生祀は1671年、京都の自邸の垂加霊社に成立したものである。これ以後も、[[Shinto|神道]]家や平田派の[[Kokugaku|国学者]]によって、それぞれ独自の祭式で自己の霊魂を祀った。
生祀の研究は加藤玄智（[[1873|1873年]] - [[1965|1965年]]）が有名である。加藤によれば判明している生祀は670あり、新しい生祀は[[1931|1931年]]8月、北海道日高国浦河町に祀られた西忠義(西義一の父。)のものである。
[[Emperor Meiji|明治天皇]]の生祀は[[1876|1876年]]（[[Meiji (era)|明治]]9年）宮城県石巻港に、[[1893|1893年]]（明治26年）[[Kamiina District, Nagano|上伊那郡]]小野村の矢彦神社境内に、建立された。明治天皇、[[Empress Shōken|昭憲皇太后]]の生祀は[[1887|1887年]]（明治20年）および[[1904|1904年]]（明治37年）に2箇所に、[[Emperor Taishō|大正天皇]]の生祀は[[1911|1911年]]（明治44年）[[Hokkaido|北海道]][[Hidaka|日高]]国門別村に、[[Empress Teimei|貞明皇后]]の生祀は[[1913|1913年]]（[[Taishō|大正]]2年）広島県広瀬町に、[[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|秩父宮雍仁親王]]、[[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu|高松宮宣仁親王]]の生祀は[[1924|1924年]]（大正13年）[[Himeji|姫路市]]同心町に成立した。
[[Hirohito|昭和天皇]]の生祀は[[1923|1923年]]（大正12年）北海道[[Tokachi|十勝]]国本別村義経山上に、昭和天皇、[[Empress Kōjun|香淳皇后]]の生祀は[[1928|1928年]]（昭和3年）広島市広瀬町に成立した。

revision.comment: /* 日本における生祠 */
revision.text:
Seishi is to enshrine the spirit of a living person. It is also called a living shrine. There are two types of enshrinement: enshrining the spirit of a living person who has made a significant contribution, and enshrining one's own spirit. [[File:Hiraga_Gennai_seishi01s2040.jpg|thumb|200x200px|[[Hiraga Gennai|平賀源内]]生祠（[[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]][[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|福山市]]鞆町）]]
== Overview ==
=== Sheng shrines in China === When [[Han Dynasty#Western Han|Former Han]]'s Reunobu was the prime minister of [[Yan Kingdom (Han dynasty)|Yan]], he built his shrine between Yan and [[Qi (state)|Qi]] and called it the "Reun Gong Shrine". Also, when [[:zh:Shiqing]] was the prime minister of Qi, the Qi people built the "Shiqing Shrine". This is the beginning of the birth shrine.<ref>[[Zhao Yi]], "The New New Ancestral Hall", Volume 32, "Ancestral Hall", "This is the beginning of the establishment of an ancestral hall.</ref>
In the [[Tang dynasty]], there were certain restrictions on the construction of monuments and shrines by officials in office. According to the [[Tang Code|Tang Rulings and Ordinances]], those who erected shrines or monuments without permission were subject to a penalty of one year of imprisonment.<ref>The [[Tang Code]], Volume 11 (The Law of Office)</ref>。The Tang Dynasty called it "the difficulty of building monuments and shrines on the first day.
In the [[Ming dynasty|Ming]], peasants in [[Tengzhou|Tengzhou]] built a shrine to commemorate [[:zh:Zhao Bangqing]], who was appointed to Yanjing for his clean politics. The shrine was built to commemorate him.
During the reign of the [[Tianqi Emperor]] of the Ming Dynasty, [[Wei Zhongxian]], the eunuch [[:zh:存禮監]] [[:zh:秉筆太監]], ruled the world by conducting his own politics, and those who flattered him or feared his authority were given a He made a living shrine for him. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing (1627), Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote: "Confucius wrote the Spring and Autumn, and Chung-Hsien wrote the Essentials. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote that Confucius had written the "Spring and Autumn" and Chung-Hsien had written the "Essentials. In April of the 7th year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, [[Yuan Chonghuan]] and [[:zh:Yan Mingtai]] of the [[Ministry of War (imperial China)|Shohshoh of the Ministry of War]] made an appeal to the government to praise the merits and demerits of Wei Zhongxian and to establish a living shrine for him in both Qiantu.<ref>『明熹宗実録』："天啓七年四月“薊遼總督閻鳴泰、巡撫袁崇煥疏，頌魏忠賢功德，請於寧前建祠，賜名懋德。」袁崇煥疏稱:「廠臣魏忠賢功在社稷，海內之共見共聞，業已銘刻金石，無容職贅，至其身任遼事，誓□恢復，梟滅逆虜，任用劉應乾、陶文、紀用等，而關內外禦敵之伏甲軍器馬匹懸簾等項，俱以家資置辦，日逐解來，又助軍需。臣方一意巡緝，嚴警諸營將吏，不敢貪懦營私，不敢餽遺隱串，改虛為實，化賈為真，易怯為勇，以有今日。浞古內臣誰有出其右者！"（『明熹宗七年都察院実録』天啓六年十月）(Is this Chinese? It is from Japanese Wikipedia so I am confused as to translation)</ref>。After that, Wei Zhongxian's raw shrine was "all over the world", "the cost of each shrine was at most hundreds of thousands, at least tens of thousands", "stolen the people's fortune, invaded the public safe, and was cut down. There are countless trees. " When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|Gu Yanwu]] lamented, "In this generation, there is no official who does not build a living shrine. And within a few years of leaving office, he would destroy the statues and change their masters. He said.<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== Shosha in Japan === Shosha, the worship of one's own spirit, was performed in order to obtain a long life or to become a god after death. It is said to have originated from the legend of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-mikoto]], who enshrined his [[Mitama|Chikishin]] and [[Mitama|Kozoshin]] in [[Mount Miwa|Sanmorozan]] ([[Mount Miwa]]).
The earliest documented case of shosha enshrining one's own spirit is that of Haruhiko Matsuki ([[824|824]]), a [[Shinkan (official) priest]] of the outer shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[923|923]] of the [[Heian period]]. - [[924|924 years]]), enshrined and enshrined his own spirit on a stone in Obe, Ise [[Watarai District, Mie|Tokai County]]. In the [[Edo period]], [[Matsudaira Sadanobu|Matsudaira Sadanobu]] established his own nascent shrine in [[1797|1797]] at Shirakawa Castle in Oshu. The number of shosshiki increased during the Edo period, probably due to the influence of Chinese thought. In the [[Edo period]], [[Yamazaki Ansai|Yakusai Yamazaki]] devised a [[Festival]] to enshrine his own spirit, referring to the rituals of [[Confucianism]]. The Shusei ritual was established in 1671 at the Tarukarei Shrine in his residence in Kyoto. After this, the [[Shinto]] family and the [[Kokugaku]] scholars of the Hirata school enshrined their own spirits in their own unique rituals.
Kato Genchi ([[1873|1873]] - [[1965|1965]]) is famous for his research on Shusei. According to Kato, there are 670 known Shuso enshrines, and the newest one is that of Nishi Tadayoshi (father of Nishi Yoshikazu, who was enshrined in Urakawa-cho, Hidaka Province, Hokkaido in August [[1931|1931]]). This is the one of [[Emperor Meiji
[Emperor Meiji]] was enshrined at Ishinomaki Port, Miyagi Prefecture in [[1876|1876]] ([[Meiji (era)|明治]]9), and at Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village, [[Kamiina District, Nagano|Kamiina County]] in [[1893|1893]] Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village. Emperor Meiji and [[Empress Shōken|Emperor Shōken]] were enshrined in two places in [[1887|1887]] and [[1904|1904]], and [[Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]] was enshrined in [[1911|1911]] in [[1944]]. [[Emperor Taishō|Taisho]] was enshrined at two locations in [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka]], [[Kadobetsu]], [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka]], [[Empress Teimei|Emperor Teimei]] was enshrined in [[1913|1913]], [[Taishō|Taisho]], [[Hirose-cho, Hiroshima Prefecture]], and [[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu Prince Chichibu]] and [[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu]] were established in [[1924|1924]] (Taisho 13) in [[Himeji|Himeji City]], Doshin-cho.
The birthplace of [[Emperor Hirohito|Showa]] was established in [[1923|1923]] (Taisho 12) in Yoshitsune, Honbetsu Village, [[Tokachi|Tokachi]], Hokkaido, and the birthplace of Emperor Showa and [[Empress Kōjun|Empress Kōjun]] was established in [[1928|1928]] (Showa 3) in Hirose-cho, Hiroshima City.

According to Kato, there are 670 known Shuso enshrines, and the newest one is that of Nishi Tadayoshi (father of Nishi Yoshikazu, who was enshrined in Urakawa-cho, Hidaka Province, Hokkaido in August [[1931|1931]]). This is the one of [[Emperor Meiji
[Emperor Meiji]] was enshrined at Ishinomaki Port, Miyagi Prefecture in [[1876|1876]] ([[Meiji (era)|明治]]9), and at Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village, [[Kamiina District, Nagano|Kamiina County]] in [[1893|1893]] Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village. Emperor Meiji and [[Empress Shōken|Emperor Shōken]] were enshrined in two places in [[1887|1887]] and [[1904|1904]], and [[Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]] was enshrined in [[1911|1911]] in [[1944]]. [[Emperor Taishō|Taisho]] was enshrined at two locations in [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka]], [[Kadobetsu]], [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka]], [[Empress Teimei|Emperor Teimei]] was enshrined in [[1913|1913]], [[Taishō|Taisho]], [[Hirose-cho, Hiroshima Prefecture]], and [[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|Prince Chichibu]] and [[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu]] were established in [[1924|1924]] (Taisho 13) in [[Himeji|Himeji City]], Doshin-cho.
The birthplace of [[Emperor Hirohito|Showa]] was established in [[1923|1923]] (Taisho 12) in Yoshitsune, Honbetsu Village, [[Tokachi|Tokachi]], Hokkaido, and the birthplace of Emperor Showa and [[Empress Kōjun|Empress Kōjun]] was established in [[1928|1928]] (Showa 3) in Hirose-cho, Hiroshima City.

[[Category:Shinto]]

Seishi is to enshrine the spirit of a living person. It is also called a living shrine. There are two types of enshrinement: enshrining the spirit of a living person who has made a significant contribution, and enshrining one's own spirit. [[File:Hiraga_Gennai_seishi01s2040.jpg|thumb|200x200px|[[Hiraga Gennai|Hiraga Gennai]]'s birth shrine in [[Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], Tomomachi.]]
== Overview ==
=== Sheng shrines in China === When [[Han Dynasty#Western Han|Former Han]]'s Reunobu was the prime minister of [[Yan Kingdom (Han dynasty)|Yan]], he built his shrine between Yan and [[Qi (state)|Qi]] and called it the "Reun Gong Shrine". Also, when [[:zh:Shiqing]] was the prime minister of Qi, the Qi people built the "Shiqing Shrine". This is the beginning of the birth shrine.<ref>[[Zhao Yi]], "The New New Ancestral Hall", Volume 32, "Ancestral Hall", "This is the beginning of the establishment of an ancestral hall.</ref>
In the [[Tang dynasty]], there were certain restrictions on the construction of monuments and shrines by officials in office. According to the [[Tang Code|Tang Rulings and Ordinances]], those who erected shrines or monuments without permission were subject to a penalty of one year of imprisonment.<ref>The [[Tang Code]], Volume 11 (The Law of Office)</ref>。The Tang Dynasty called it "the difficulty of building monuments and shrines on the first day.
In the [[Ming dynasty|Ming]], peasants in [[Tengzhou|Tengzhou]] built a shrine to commemorate [[:zh:Zhao Bangqing]], who was appointed to Yanjing for his clean politics. The shrine was built to commemorate him.
During the reign of the [[Tianqi Emperor]] of the Ming Dynasty, [[Wei Zhongxian]], the eunuch [[:zh:存禮監]] [[:zh:秉筆太監]], ruled the world by conducting his own politics, and those who flattered him or feared his authority were given a He made a living shrine for him. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing (1627), Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote: "Confucius wrote the Spring and Autumn, and Chung-Hsien wrote the Essentials. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote that Confucius had written the "Spring and Autumn" and Chung-Hsien had written the "Essentials. In April of the 7th year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, [[Yuan Chonghuan]] and [[:zh:Yan Mingtai]] of the [[Ministry of War (imperial China)|Shohshoh of the Ministry of War]] made an appeal to the government to praise the merits and demerits of Wei Zhongxian and to establish a living shrine for him in both Qiantu.<ref>『明熹宗実録』："天啓七年四月“薊遼總督閻鳴泰、巡撫袁崇煥疏，頌魏忠賢功德，請於寧前建祠，賜名懋德。」袁崇煥疏稱:「廠臣魏忠賢功在社稷，海內之共見共聞，業已銘刻金石，無容職贅，至其身任遼事，誓□恢復，梟滅逆虜，任用劉應乾、陶文、紀用等，而關內外禦敵之伏甲軍器馬匹懸簾等項，俱以家資置辦，日逐解來，又助軍需。臣方一意巡緝，嚴警諸營將吏，不敢貪懦營私，不敢餽遺隱串，改虛為實，化賈為真，易怯為勇，以有今日。浞古內臣誰有出其右者！"（『明熹宗七年都察院実録』天啓六年十月）(Is this Chinese? It is from Japanese Wikipedia so I am confused as to translation)</ref>。After that, Wei Zhongxian's raw shrine was "all over the world", "the cost of each shrine was at most hundreds of thousands, at least tens of thousands", "stolen the people's fortune, invaded the public safe, and was cut down. There are countless trees. " When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|Gu Yanwu]] lamented, "In this generation, there is no official who does not build a living shrine. And within a few years of leaving office, he would destroy the statues and change their masters. He said.<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== Shosha in Japan === Shosha, the worship of one's own spirit, was performed in order to obtain a long life or to become a god after death. It is said to have originated from the legend of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-mikoto]], who enshrined his [[Mitama|Chikishin]] and [[Mitama|Kozoshin]] in [[Mount Miwa|Sanmorozan]] ([[Mount Miwa]]).
The earliest documented case of shosha enshrining one's own spirit is that of Haruhiko Matsuki ([[824|824]]), a [[Shinkan (official) priest]] of the outer shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[923|923]] of the [[Heian period]]. - [[924|924 years]]), enshrined and enshrined his own spirit on a stone in Obe, Ise [[Watarai District, Mie|Tokai County]]. In the [[Edo period]], [[Matsudaira Sadanobu|Matsudaira Sadanobu]] established his own nascent shrine in [[1797|1797]] at Shirakawa Castle in Oshu. The number of shosshiki increased during the Edo period, probably due to the influence of Chinese thought. In the [[Edo period]], [[Yamazaki Ansai|Yakusai Yamazaki]] devised a [[Festival]] to enshrine his own spirit, referring to the rituals of [[Confucianism]]. The Shusei ritual was established in 1671 at the Tarukarei Shrine in his residence in Kyoto. After this, the [[Shinto]] family and the [[Kokugaku]] scholars of the Hirata school enshrined their own spirits in their own unique rituals.
Kato Genchi ([[1873|1873]] - [[1965|1965]]) is famous for his research on Shusei. According to Kato, there are 670 known Shuso enshrines, and the newest one is that of Nishi Tadayoshi (father of Nishi Yoshikazu, who was enshrined in Urakawa-cho, Hidaka Province, Hokkaido in August [[1931|1931]]). This is the one of [[Emperor Meiji
[Emperor Meiji]] was enshrined at Ishinomaki Port, Miyagi Prefecture in [[1876|1876]] ([[Meiji (era)|明治]]9), and at Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village, [[Kamiina District, Nagano|Kamiina County]] in [[1893|1893]] Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village. Emperor Meiji and [[Empress Shōken|Emperor Shōken]] were enshrined in two places in [[1887|1887]] and [[1904|1904]], and [[Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]] was enshrined in [[1911|1911]] in [[1944]]. [[Emperor Taishō|Taisho]] was enshrined at two locations in [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka]], [[Kadobetsu]], [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka]], [[Empress Teimei|Emperor Teimei]] was enshrined in [[1913|1913]], [[Taishō|Taisho]], [[Hirose-cho, Hiroshima Prefecture]], and [[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|Prince Chichibu]] and [[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu]] were established in [[1924|1924]] (Taisho 13) in [[Himeji|Himeji City]], Doshin-cho.
The birthplace of [[Emperor Hirohito|Showa]] was established in [[1923|1923]] (Taisho 12) in Yoshitsune, Honbetsu Village, [[Tokachi|Tokachi]], Hokkaido, and the birthplace of Emperor Showa and [[Empress Kōjun|Empress Kōjun]] was established in [[1928|1928]] (Showa 3) in Hirose-cho, Hiroshima City.

This is the beginning of the birth shrine.<ref>[[Zhao Yi]], "The New New Ancestral Hall", Volume 32, "Ancestral Hall", "This is the beginning of the establishment of an ancestral hall.</ref>
In the [[Tang dynasty]], there were certain restrictions on the construction of monuments and shrines by officials in office. According to the [[Tang Code|Tang Rulings and Ordinances]], those who erected shrines or monuments without permission were subject to a penalty of one year of imprisonment.<ref>The [[Tang Code]], Volume 11 (The Law of Office)</ref>。The Tang Dynasty called it "the difficulty of building monuments and shrines on the first day.
In the [[Ming dynasty|Ming]], peasants in [[Tengzhou|Tengzhou]] built a shrine to commemorate [[:zh:趙邦清|Zhao Bangqing]], who was appointed to Yanjing for his clean politics. The shrine was built to commemorate him.
During the reign of the [[Tianqi Emperor]] of the Ming Dynasty, [[Wei Zhongxian]], the eunuch [[:zh:存禮監]] [[:zh:秉筆太監]], ruled the world by conducting his own politics, and those who flattered him or feared his authority were given a He made a living shrine for him. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing (1627), Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote: "Confucius wrote the Spring and Autumn, and Chung-Hsien wrote the Essentials. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote that Confucius had written the "Spring and Autumn" and Chung-Hsien had written the "Essentials. In April of the 7th year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, [[Yuan Chonghuan]] and [[:zh:閻鳴泰|Yan Mingtai]] of the [[Ministry of War (imperial China)|Shohshoh of the Ministry of War]] made an appeal to the government to praise the merits and demerits of Wei Zhongxian and to establish a living shrine for him in both Qiantu.<ref>『明熹宗実録』："天啓七年四月“薊遼總督閻鳴泰、巡撫袁崇煥疏，頌魏忠賢功德，請於寧前建祠，賜名懋德。」袁崇煥疏稱:「廠臣魏忠賢功在社稷，海內之共見共聞，業已銘刻金石，無容職贅，至其身任遼事，誓□恢復，梟滅逆虜，任用劉應乾、陶文、紀用等，而關內外禦敵之伏甲軍器馬匹懸簾等項，俱以家資置辦，日逐解來，又助軍需。臣方一意巡緝，嚴警諸營將吏，不敢貪懦營私，不敢餽遺隱串，改虛為實，化賈為真，易怯為勇，以有今日。浞古內臣誰有出其右者！"（『明熹宗七年都察院実録』天啓六年十月）(Is this Chinese? It is from Japanese Wikipedia so I am confused as to translation)</ref>。After that, Wei Zhongxian's raw shrine was "all over the world", "the cost of each shrine was at most hundreds of thousands, at least tens of thousands", "stolen the people's fortune, invaded the public safe, and was cut down. There are countless trees. " When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|Gu Yanwu]] lamented, "In this generation, there is no official who does not build a living shrine. And within a few years of leaving office, he would destroy the statues and change their masters. He said.<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== Shosha in Japan === Shosha, the worship of one's own spirit, was performed in order to obtain a long life or to become a god after death. It is said to have originated from the legend of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-mikoto]], who enshrined his [[Mitama|Chikishin]] and [[Mitama|Kozoshin]] in [[Mount Miwa|Sanmorozan]] ([[Mount Miwa]]).
The earliest documented case of shosha enshrining one's own spirit is that of Haruhiko Matsuki ([[824|824]]), a [[Shinkan (official) priest]] of the outer shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[923|923]] of the [[Heian period]]. - [[924|924 years]]), enshrined and enshrined his own spirit on a stone in Obe, Ise [[Watarai District, Mie|Tokai County]]. In the [[Edo period]], [[Matsudaira Sadanobu|Matsudaira Sadanobu]] established his own nascent shrine in [[1797|1797]] at Shirakawa Castle in Oshu. The number of shosshiki increased during the Edo period, probably due to the influence of Chinese thought. In the [[Edo period]], [[Yamazaki Ansai|Yakusai Yamazaki]] devised a [[Festival]] to enshrine his own spirit, referring to the rituals of [[Confucianism]]. The Shusei ritual was established in 1671 at the Tarukarei Shrine in his residence in Kyoto. After this, the [[Shinto]] family and the [[Kokugaku]] scholars of the Hirata school enshrined their own spirits in their own unique rituals.
Kato Genchi ([[1873|1873]] - [[1965|1965]]) is famous for his research on Shusei. According to Kato, there are 670 known Shuso enshrines, and the newest one is that of Nishi Tadayoshi (father of Nishi Yoshikazu, who was enshrined in Urakawa-cho, Hidaka Province, Hokkaido in August [[1931|1931]]). This is the one of [[Emperor Meiji]]
[[Emperor Meiji]] was enshrined at Ishinomaki Port, Miyagi Prefecture in [[1876|1876]] ([[Meiji (era)|明治]]9), and at Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village, [[Kamiina District, Nagano|Kamiina County]] in [[1893|1893]] Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village. Emperor Meiji and [[Empress Shōken|Emperor Shōken]] were enshrined in two places in [[1887|1887]] and [[1904|1904]], and [[Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]] was enshrined in [[1911|1911]] in [[1944]]. [[Emperor Taishō|Taisho]] was enshrined at two locations in [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka]], [[Kadobetsu]], [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka]], [[Empress Teimei|Emperor Teimei]] was enshrined in [[1913|1913]], [[Taishō|Taisho]], [[Hirose-cho, Hiroshima Prefecture]], and [[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|Prince Chichibu]] and [[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu]] were established in [[1924|1924]] (Taisho 13) in [[Himeji|Himeji City]], Doshin-cho.
The birthplace of [[Emperor Hirohito|Showa]] was established in [[1923|1923]] (Taisho 12) in Yoshitsune, Honbetsu Village, [[Tokachi|Tokachi]], Hokkaido, and the birthplace of Emperor Showa and [[Empress Kōjun|Empress Kōjun]] was established in [[1928|1928]] (Showa 3) in Hirose-cho, Hiroshima City.

Seishi is to enshrine the spirit of a living person. It is also called a living shrine. There are two types of enshrinement: enshrining the spirit of a living person who has made a significant contribution, and enshrining one's own spirit. [[File:Hiraga_Gennai_seishi01s2040.jpg|thumb|200x200px|[[Hiraga Gennai|Hiraga Gennai]]'s birth shrine in [[Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], Tomomachi.]]
== Overview ==
=== Sheng shrines in China === When [[Han Dynasty#Western Han|Former Han]]'s Reunobu was the prime minister of [[Yan Kingdom (Han dynasty)|Yan]], he built his shrine between Yan and [[Qi (state)|Qi]] and called it the "Reun Gong Shrine". Also, when [[:zh:石慶|Shiqing]] was the prime minister of Qi, the Qi people built the "Shiqing Shrine". This is the beginning of the birth shrine.<ref>[[Zhao Yi]], "The New New Ancestral Hall", Volume 32, "Ancestral Hall", "This is the beginning of the establishment of an ancestral hall.</ref>
In the [[Tang dynasty]], there were certain restrictions on the construction of monuments and shrines by officials in office. According to the [[Tang Code|Tang Rulings and Ordinances]], those who erected shrines or monuments without permission were subject to a penalty of one year of imprisonment.<ref>The [[Tang Code]], Volume 11 (The Law of Office)</ref>。The Tang Dynasty called it "the difficulty of building monuments and shrines on the first day.
In the [[Ming dynasty|Ming]], peasants in [[Tengzhou|Tengzhou]] built a shrine to commemorate [[:zh:趙邦清|Zhao Bangqing]], who was appointed to Yanjing for his clean politics. The shrine was built to commemorate him.

In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing (1627), Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote: "Confucius wrote the Spring and Autumn, and Chung-Hsien wrote the Essentials. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote that Confucius had written the "Spring and Autumn" and Chung-Hsien had written the "Essentials. In April of the 7th year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, [[Yuan Chonghuan]] and [[:zh:閻鳴泰|Yan Mingtai]] of the [[Ministry of War (imperial China)|Shohshoh of the Ministry of War]] made an appeal to the government to praise the merits and demerits of Wei Zhongxian and to establish a living shrine for him in both Qiantu.<ref>『明熹宗実録』："天啓七年四月“薊遼總督閻鳴泰、巡撫袁崇煥疏，頌魏忠賢功德，請於寧前建祠，賜名懋德。」袁崇煥疏稱:「廠臣魏忠賢功在社稷，海內之共見共聞，業已銘刻金石，無容職贅，至其身任遼事，誓□恢復，梟滅逆虜，任用劉應乾、陶文、紀用等，而關內外禦敵之伏甲軍器馬匹懸簾等項，俱以家資置辦，日逐解來，又助軍需。臣方一意巡緝，嚴警諸營將吏，不敢貪懦營私，不敢餽遺隱串，改虛為實，化賈為真，易怯為勇，以有今日。浞古內臣誰有出其右者！"（『明熹宗七年都察院実録』天啓六年十月）(Is this Chinese? It is from Japanese Wikipedia so I am confused as to translation)</ref>。After that, Wei Zhongxian's raw shrine was "all over the world", "the cost of each shrine was at most hundreds of thousands, at least tens of thousands", "stolen the people's fortune, invaded the public safe, and was cut down. There are countless trees. " When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|Gu Yanwu]] lamented, "In this generation, there is no official who does not build a living shrine. And within a few years of leaving office, he would destroy the statues and change their masters. He said.<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== Shosha in Japan === Shosha, the worship of one's own spirit, was performed in order to obtain a long life or to become a god after death. It is said to have originated from the legend of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-mikoto]], who enshrined his [[Mitama|Chikishin]] and [[Mitama|Kozoshin]] in [[Mount Miwa|Sanmorozan]] ([[Mount Miwa]]).
The earliest documented case of shosha enshrining one's own spirit is that of Haruhiko Matsuki ([[824|824]]), a [[Shinkan (official) priest]] of the outer shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[923|923]] of the [[Heian period]]. - [[924|924 years]]), enshrined and enshrined his own spirit on a stone in Obe, Ise [[Watarai District, Mie|Tokai County]]. In the [[Edo period]], [[Matsudaira Sadanobu|Matsudaira Sadanobu]] established his own nascent shrine in [[1797|1797]] at Shirakawa Castle in Oshu. The number of shosshiki increased during the Edo period, probably due to the influence of Chinese thought. In the [[Edo period]], [[Yamazaki Ansai|Yakusai Yamazaki]] devised a [[Festival]] to enshrine his own spirit, referring to the rituals of [[Confucianism]]. This is the one of [[Emperor Meiji]]
[[Emperor Meiji]] was enshrined at Ishinomaki Port, Miyagi Prefecture in [[1876|1876]] ([[Meiji (era)|明治]]9), and at Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village, [[Kamiina District, Nagano|Kamiina County]] in [[1893|1893]] Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village. Emperor Meiji and [[Empress Shōken|Emperor Shōken]] were enshrined in two places in [[1887|1887]] and [[1904|1904]], and [[Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]] was enshrined in [[1911|1911]] in [[1944]]. [[Emperor Taishō|Taisho]] was enshrined at two locations in [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka]], [[Kadobetsu]], [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka]], [[Empress Teimei|Emperor Teimei]] was enshrined in [[1913|1913]], [[Taishō|Taisho]], [[Hirose-cho, Hiroshima Prefecture]], and [[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|Prince Chichibu]] and [[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu]] were established in [[1924|1924]] (Taisho 13) in [[Himeji|Himeji City]], Doshin-cho.
The birthplace of [[Emperor Hirohito|Showa]] was established in [[1923|1923]] (Taisho 12) in Yoshitsune, Honbetsu Village, [[Tokachi|Tokachi]], Hokkaido, and the birthplace of Emperor Showa and [[Empress Kōjun|Empress Kōjun]] was established in [[1928|1928]] (Showa 3) in Hirose-cho, Hiroshima City.

In April of the 7th year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, [[Yuan Chonghuan]] and [[:zh:閻鳴泰|Yan Mingtai]] of the [[Ministry of War (imperial China)|Shohshoh of the Ministry of War]] made an appeal to the government to praise the merits and demerits of Wei Zhongxian and to establish a living shrine for him in both Qiantu.<ref>『明熹宗実録』："天啓七年四月“薊遼總督閻鳴泰、巡撫袁崇煥疏，頌魏忠賢功德，請於寧前建祠，賜名懋德。」袁崇煥疏稱:「廠臣魏忠賢功在社稷，海內之共見共聞，業已銘刻金石，無容職贅，至其身任遼事，誓□恢復，梟滅逆虜，任用劉應乾、陶文、紀用等，而關內外禦敵之伏甲軍器馬匹懸簾等項，俱以家資置辦，日逐解來，又助軍需。臣方一意巡緝，嚴警諸營將吏，不敢貪懦營私，不敢餽遺隱串，改虛為實，化賈為真，易怯為勇，以有今日。浞古內臣誰有出其右者！"（『明熹宗七年都察院実録』天啓六年十月）<!--Is this Chinese? It is from Japanese Wikipedia so I am confused as to translation--></ref>。After that, Wei Zhongxian's raw shrine was "all over the world", "the cost of each shrine was at most hundreds of thousands, at least tens of thousands", "stolen the people's fortune, invaded the public safe, and was cut down. There are countless trees. " When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|Gu Yanwu]] lamented, "In this generation, there is no official who does not build a living shrine. And within a few years of leaving office, he would destroy the statues and change their masters. He said.<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== Shosha in Japan === Shosha, the worship of one's own spirit, was performed in order to obtain a long life or to become a god after death. It is said to have originated from the legend of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-mikoto]], who enshrined his [[Mitama|Chikishin]] and [[Mitama|Kozoshin]] in [[Mount Miwa|Sanmorozan]] ([[Mount Miwa]]).
The earliest documented case of shosha enshrining one's own spirit is that of Haruhiko Matsuki ([[824|824]]), a [[Shinkan (official) priest]] of the outer shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[923|923]] of the [[Heian period]]. - [[924|924 years]]), enshrined and enshrined his own spirit on a stone in Obe, Ise [[Watarai District, Mie|Tokai County]]. In the [[Edo period]], [[Matsudaira Sadanobu|Matsudaira Sadanobu]] established his own nascent shrine in [[1797|1797]] at Shirakawa Castle in Oshu. The number of shosshiki increased during the Edo period, probably due to the influence of Chinese thought. In the [[Edo period]], [[Yamazaki Ansai|Yakusai Yamazaki]] devised a [[Festival]] to enshrine his own spirit, referring to the rituals of [[Confucianism]]. The Shusei ritual was established in 1671 at the Tarukarei Shrine in his residence in Kyoto. After this, the [[Shinto]] family and the [[Kokugaku]] scholars of the Hirata school enshrined their own spirits in their own unique rituals.
Kato Genchi ([[1873|1873]] - [[1965|1965]]) is famous for his research on Shusei. According to Kato, there are 670 known Shuso enshrines, and the newest one is that of Nishi Tadayoshi (father of Nishi Yoshikazu, who was enshrined in Urakawa-cho, Hidaka Province, Hokkaido in August [[1931|1931]]). This is the one of [[Emperor Meiji]]
[[Emperor Meiji]] was enshrined at Ishinomaki Port, Miyagi Prefecture in [[1876|1876]] ([[Meiji (era)|明治]]9), and at Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village, [[Kamiina District, Nagano|Kamiina County]] in [[1893|1893]] Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village. Emperor Meiji and [[Empress Shōken|Emperor Shōken]] were enshrined in two places in [[1887|1887]] and [[1904|1904]], and [[Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]] was enshrined in [[1911|1911]] in [[1944]]. [[Emperor Taishō|Taisho]] was enshrined at two locations in [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka, Hokkaidō|Hidaka]], [[Kadobetsu]], [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka, Hokkaidō|Hidaka]], [[Empress Teimei|Emperor Teimei]] was enshrined in [[1913|1913]], [[Taishō|Taisho]], [[Hirose-cho, Hiroshima Prefecture]], and [[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|Prince Chichibu]] and [[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu]] were established in [[1924|1924]] (Taisho 13) in [[Himeji|Himeji City]], Doshin-cho.
The birthplace of [[Emperor Hirohito|Showa]] was established in [[1923|1923]] (Taisho 12) in Yoshitsune, Honbetsu Village, [[Tokachi District, Hokkaido|Tokachi]], Hokkaido, and the birthplace of Emperor Showa and [[Empress Kōjun|Empress Kōjun]] was established in [[1928|1928]] (Showa 3) in Hirose-cho, Hiroshima City.
== References == <references />
== See Also ==

[[Category:Shinto]]

revision.comment: Onel5969 moved page [[Sheng Shrine]] to [[Draft:Sheng Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: [[WP:DRAFTIFY|Not ready]] for mainspace, incubate in draftspace ([[User:Evad37/MoveToDraft.js|via script]])
revision.text:
Seishi is to enshrine the spirit of a living person. It is also called a living shrine. There are two types of enshrinement: enshrining the spirit of a living person who has made a significant contribution, and enshrining one's own spirit. [[File:Hiraga_Gennai_seishi01s2040.jpg|thumb|200x200px|[[Hiraga Gennai|Hiraga Gennai]]'s birth shrine in [[Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], Tomomachi.]]
== Overview ==
=== Sheng shrines in China === When [[Han Dynasty#Western Han|Former Han]]'s Reunobu was the prime minister of [[Yan Kingdom (Han dynasty)|Yan]], he built his shrine between Yan and [[Qi (state)|Qi]] and called it the "Reun Gong Shrine". Also, when [[:zh:石慶|Shiqing]] was the prime minister of Qi, the Qi people built the "Shiqing Shrine". This is the beginning of the birth shrine.<ref>[[Zhao Yi]], "The New New Ancestral Hall", Volume 32, "Ancestral Hall", "This is the beginning of the establishment of an ancestral hall.</ref>
In the [[Tang dynasty]], there were certain restrictions on the construction of monuments and shrines by officials in office. According to the [[Tang Code|Tang Rulings and Ordinances]], those who erected shrines or monuments without permission were subject to a penalty of one year of imprisonment.<ref>The [[Tang Code]], Volume 11 (The Law of Office)</ref>。The Tang Dynasty called it "the difficulty of building monuments and shrines on the first day.
In the [[Ming dynasty|Ming]], peasants in [[Tengzhou|Tengzhou]] built a shrine to commemorate [[:zh:趙邦清|Zhao Bangqing]], who was appointed to Yanjing for his clean politics. The shrine was built to commemorate him.
During the reign of the [[Tianqi Emperor]] of the Ming Dynasty, [[Wei Zhongxian]], the eunuch [[:zh:存禮監]] [[:zh:秉筆太監]], ruled the world by conducting his own politics, and those who flattered him or feared his authority were given a He made a living shrine for him. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing (1627), Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote: "Confucius wrote the Spring and Autumn, and Chung-Hsien wrote the Essentials. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote that Confucius had written the "Spring and Autumn" and Chung-Hsien had written the "Essentials. In April of the 7th year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, [[Yuan Chonghuan]] and [[:zh:閻鳴泰|Yan Mingtai]] of the [[Ministry of War (imperial China)|Shohshoh of the Ministry of War]] made an appeal to the government to praise the merits and demerits of Wei Zhongxian and to establish a living shrine for him in both Qiantu.<ref>『明熹宗実録』："天啓七年四月“薊遼總督閻鳴泰、巡撫袁崇煥疏，頌魏忠賢功德，請於寧前建祠，賜名懋德。」袁崇煥疏稱:「廠臣魏忠賢功在社稷，海內之共見共聞，業已銘刻金石，無容職贅，至其身任遼事，誓□恢復，梟滅逆虜，任用劉應乾、陶文、紀用等，而關內外禦敵之伏甲軍器馬匹懸簾等項，俱以家資置辦，日逐解來，又助軍需。臣方一意巡緝，嚴警諸營將吏，不敢貪懦營私，不敢餽遺隱串，改虛為實，化賈為真，易怯為勇，以有今日。浞古內臣誰有出其右者！"（『明熹宗七年都察院実録』天啓六年十月）<!--Is this Chinese? It is from Japanese Wikipedia so I am confused as to translation--></ref>。After that, Wei Zhongxian's raw shrine was "all over the world", "the cost of each shrine was at most hundreds of thousands, at least tens of thousands", "stolen the people's fortune, invaded the public safe, and was cut down. There are countless trees. " When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|Gu Yanwu]] lamented, "In this generation, there is no official who does not build a living shrine. And within a few years of leaving office, he would destroy the statues and change their masters. He said.<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== Shosha in Japan === Shosha, the worship of one's own spirit, was performed in order to obtain a long life or to become a god after death. It is said to have originated from the legend of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-mikoto]], who enshrined his [[Mitama|Chikishin]] and [[Mitama|Kozoshin]] in [[Mount Miwa|Sanmorozan]] ([[Mount Miwa]]).
The earliest documented case of shosha enshrining one's own spirit is that of Haruhiko Matsuki ([[824|824]]), a [[Shinkan (official) priest]] of the outer shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[923|923]] of the [[Heian period]]. - [[924|924 years]]), enshrined and enshrined his own spirit on a stone in Obe, Ise [[Watarai District, Mie|Tokai County]]. In the [[Edo period]], [[Matsudaira Sadanobu|Matsudaira Sadanobu]] established his own nascent shrine in [[1797|1797]] at Shirakawa Castle in Oshu. The number of shosshiki increased during the Edo period, probably due to the influence of Chinese thought. In the [[Edo period]], [[Yamazaki Ansai|Yakusai Yamazaki]] devised a [[Festival]] to enshrine his own spirit, referring to the rituals of [[Confucianism]]. The Shusei ritual was established in 1671 at the Tarukarei Shrine in his residence in Kyoto. After this, the [[Shinto]] family and the [[Kokugaku]] scholars of the Hirata school enshrined their own spirits in their own unique rituals.
Kato Genchi ([[1873|1873]] - [[1965|1965]]) is famous for his research on Shusei. According to Kato, there are 670 known Shuso enshrines, and the newest one is that of Nishi Tadayoshi (father of Nishi Yoshikazu, who was enshrined in Urakawa-cho, Hidaka Province, Hokkaido in August [[1931|1931]]). This is the one of [[Emperor Meiji]]
[[Emperor Meiji]] was enshrined at Ishinomaki Port, Miyagi Prefecture in [[1876|1876]] ([[Meiji (era)|明治]]9), and at Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village, [[Kamiina District, Nagano|Kamiina County]] in [[1893|1893]] Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village. Emperor Meiji and [[Empress Shōken|Emperor Shōken]] were enshrined in two places in [[1887|1887]] and [[1904|1904]], and [[Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]] was enshrined in [[1911|1911]] in [[1944]]. [[Emperor Taishō|Taisho]] was enshrined at two locations in [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka, Hokkaidō|Hidaka]], [[Kadobetsu]], [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka, Hokkaidō|Hidaka]], [[Empress Teimei|Emperor Teimei]] was enshrined in [[1913|1913]], [[Taishō|Taisho]], [[Hirose-cho, Hiroshima Prefecture]], and [[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|Prince Chichibu]] and [[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu]] were established in [[1924|1924]] (Taisho 13) in [[Himeji|Himeji City]], Doshin-cho.
The birthplace of [[Emperor Hirohito|Showa]] was established in [[1923|1923]] (Taisho 12) in Yoshitsune, Honbetsu Village, [[Tokachi District, Hokkaido|Tokachi]], Hokkaido, and the birthplace of Emperor Showa and [[Empress Kōjun|Empress Kōjun]] was established in [[1928|1928]] (Showa 3) in Hirose-cho, Hiroshima City.

" When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|Gu Yanwu]] lamented, "In this generation, there is no official who does not build a living shrine. And within a few years of leaving office, he would destroy the statues and change their masters. He said.<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== Shosha in Japan === Shosha, the worship of one's own spirit, was performed in order to obtain a long life or to become a god after death. It is said to have originated from the legend of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-mikoto]], who enshrined his [[Mitama|Chikishin]] and [[Mitama|Kozoshin]] in [[Mount Miwa|Sanmorozan]] ([[Mount Miwa]]).
The earliest documented case of shosha enshrining one's own spirit is that of Haruhiko Matsuki ([[824|824]]), a [[Shinkan (official) priest]] of the outer shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[923|923]] of the [[Heian period]]. - [[924|924 years]]), enshrined and enshrined his own spirit on a stone in Obe, Ise [[Watarai District, Mie|Tokai County]]. In the [[Edo period]], [[Matsudaira Sadanobu|Matsudaira Sadanobu]] established his own nascent shrine in [[1797|1797]] at Shirakawa Castle in Oshu. The number of shosshiki increased during the Edo period, probably due to the influence of Chinese thought. In the [[Edo period]], [[Yamazaki Ansai|Yakusai Yamazaki]] devised a [[Festival]] to enshrine his own spirit, referring to the rituals of [[Confucianism]]. The Shusei ritual was established in 1671 at the Tarukarei Shrine in his residence in Kyoto. After this, the [[Shinto]] family and the [[Kokugaku]] scholars of the Hirata school enshrined their own spirits in their own unique rituals.
Kato Genchi ([[1873|1873]] - [[1965|1965]]) is famous for his research on Shusei. According to Kato, there are 670 known Shuso enshrines, and the newest one is that of Nishi Tadayoshi (father of Nishi Yoshikazu, who was enshrined in Urakawa-cho, Hidaka Province, Hokkaido in August [[1931|1931]]). This is the one of [[Emperor Meiji]]
[[Emperor Meiji]] was enshrined at Ishinomaki Port, Miyagi Prefecture in [[1876|1876]] ([[Meiji (era)|明治]]9), and at Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village, [[Kamiina District, Nagano|Kamiina County]] in [[1893|1893]] Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village. Emperor Meiji and [[Empress Shōken|Emperor Shōken]] were enshrined in two places in [[1887|1887]] and [[1904|1904]], and [[Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]] was enshrined in [[1911|1911]] in [[1944]]. [[Emperor Taishō|Taisho]] was enshrined at two locations in [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka, Hokkaidō|Hidaka]], [[Kadobetsu]], [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka, Hokkaidō|Hidaka]], [[Empress Teimei|Emperor Teimei]] was enshrined in [[1913|1913]], [[Taishō|Taisho]], [[Hirose-cho, Hiroshima Prefecture]], and [[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|Prince Chichibu]] and [[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu]] were established in [[1924|1924]] (Taisho 13) in [[Himeji|Himeji City]], Doshin-cho.
The birthplace of [[Emperor Hirohito|Showa]] was established in [[1923|1923]] (Taisho 12) in Yoshitsune, Honbetsu Village, [[Tokachi District, Hokkaido|Tokachi]], Hokkaido, and the birthplace of Emperor Showa and [[Empress Kōjun|Empress Kōjun]] was established in [[1928|1928]] (Showa 3) in Hirose-cho, Hiroshima City.

{{AfC submission|t||ts=20220218132857|u=SiliconProphet|ns=118|demo=}}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects to [[Sheng_Shrine]] before draftification: *[[Seishi (Shinto)]] --> A {{ill|Sheng Shrine|ja|生祀|zh|生祠}} is a [[shrine]] for a living person. There are two types of enshrinement: enshrining the spirit of a living person who has made a significant contribution and enshrining one's own spirit. [[File:Hiraga_Gennai_seishi01s2040.jpg|thumb|200x200px|[[Hiraga Gennai|Hiraga Gennai]]'s birth shrine in [[Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], Tomomachi.]]
== Overview ==
=== Sheng shrines in China === When [[Han Dynasty#Western Han|Former Han]]'s Reunobu was the prime minister of [[Yan Kingdom (Han dynasty)|Yan]], he built his shrine between Yan and [[Qi (state)|Qi]] and called it the "Reun Gong Shrine". Also, when [[:zh:石慶|Shiqing]] was the prime minister of Qi, the Qi people built the "Shiqing Shrine". This is the beginning of the birth shrine.<ref>[[Zhao Yi]], "The New New Ancestral Hall", Volume 32, "Ancestral Hall", "This is the beginning of the establishment of an ancestral hall.</ref>
In the [[Tang dynasty]], there were certain restrictions on the construction of monuments and shrines by officials in office. According to the [[Tang Code|Tang Rulings and Ordinances]], those who erected shrines or monuments without permission were subject to a penalty of one year of imprisonment.<ref>The [[Tang Code]], Volume 11 (The Law of Office)</ref>。The Tang Dynasty called it "the difficulty of building monuments and shrines on the first day.
In the [[Ming dynasty|Ming]], peasants in [[Tengzhou|Tengzhou]] built a shrine to commemorate [[:zh:趙邦清|Zhao Bangqing]], who was appointed to Yanjing for his clean politics. The shrine was built to commemorate him.
During the reign of the [[Tianqi Emperor]] of the Ming Dynasty, [[Wei Zhongxian]], the eunuch [[:zh:存禮監]] [[:zh:秉筆太監]], ruled the world by conducting his own politics, and those who flattered him or feared his authority were given a He made a living shrine for him. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing (1627), Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote: "Confucius wrote the Spring and Autumn, and Chung-Hsien wrote the Essentials. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote that Confucius had written the "Spring and Autumn" and Chung-Hsien had written the "Essentials. In April of the 7th year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, [[Yuan Chonghuan]] and [[:zh:閻鳴泰|Yan Mingtai]] of the [[Ministry of War (imperial China)|Shohshoh of the Ministry of War]] made an appeal to the government to praise the merits and demerits of Wei Zhongxian and to establish a living shrine for him in both Qiantu.<ref>『明熹宗実録』："天啓七年四月“薊遼總督閻鳴泰、巡撫袁崇煥疏，頌魏忠賢功德，請於寧前建祠，賜名懋德。」袁崇煥疏稱:「廠臣魏忠賢功在社稷，海內之共見共聞，業已銘刻金石，無容職贅，至其身任遼事，誓□恢復，梟滅逆虜，任用劉應乾、陶文、紀用等，而關內外禦敵之伏甲軍器馬匹懸簾等項，俱以家資置辦，日逐解來，又助軍需。臣方一意巡緝，嚴警諸營將吏，不敢貪懦營私，不敢餽遺隱串，改虛為實，化賈為真，易怯為勇，以有今日。浞古內臣誰有出其右者！"（『明熹宗七年都察院実録』天啓六年十月）<!--Is this Chinese? It is from Japanese Wikipedia so I am confused as to translation--></ref>。After that, Wei Zhongxian's raw shrine was "all over the world", "the cost of each shrine was at most hundreds of thousands, at least tens of thousands", "stolen the people's fortune, invaded the public safe, and was cut down. There are countless trees. " When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|Gu Yanwu]] lamented, "In this generation, there is no official who does not build a living shrine. And within a few years of leaving office, he would destroy the statues and change their masters. He said.<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== Shosha in Japan === Shosha, the worship of one's own spirit, was performed in order to obtain a long life or to become a god after death. It is said to have originated from the legend of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-mikoto]], who enshrined his [[Mitama|Chikishin]] and [[Mitama|Kozoshin]] in [[Mount Miwa|Sanmorozan]] ([[Mount Miwa]]).
The earliest documented case of shosha enshrining one's own spirit is that of Haruhiko Matsuki ([[824|824]]), a [[Shinkan (official) priest]] of the outer shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[923|923]] of the [[Heian period]]. - [[924|924 years]]), enshrined and enshrined his own spirit on a stone in Obe, Ise [[Watarai District, Mie|Tokai County]]. In the [[Edo period]], [[Matsudaira Sadanobu|Matsudaira Sadanobu]] established his own nascent shrine in [[1797|1797]] at Shirakawa Castle in Oshu. The number of shosshiki increased during the Edo period, probably due to the influence of Chinese thought. In the [[Edo period]], [[Yamazaki Ansai|Yakusai Yamazaki]] devised a [[Festival]] to enshrine his own spirit, referring to the rituals of [[Confucianism]]. The Shusei ritual was established in 1671 at the Tarukarei Shrine in his residence in Kyoto. After this, the [[Shinto]] family and the [[Kokugaku]] scholars of the Hirata school enshrined their own spirits in their own unique rituals.
Kato Genchi ([[1873|1873]] - [[1965|1965]]) is famous for his research on Shusei. According to Kato, there are 670 known Shuso enshrines, and the newest one is that of Nishi Tadayoshi (father of Nishi Yoshikazu, who was enshrined in Urakawa-cho, Hidaka Province, Hokkaido in August [[1931|1931]]). This is the one of [[Emperor Meiji]]
[[Emperor Meiji]] was enshrined at Ishinomaki Port, Miyagi Prefecture in [[1876|1876]] ([[Meiji (era)|明治]]9), and at Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village, [[Kamiina District, Nagano|Kamiina County]] in [[1893|1893]] Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village. Emperor Meiji and [[Empress Shōken|Emperor Shōken]] were enshrined in two places in [[1887|1887]] and [[1904|1904]], and [[Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]] was enshrined in [[1911|1911]] in [[1944]]. [[Emperor Taishō|Taisho]] was enshrined at two locations in [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka, Hokkaidō|Hidaka]], [[Kadobetsu]], [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka, Hokkaidō|Hidaka]], [[Empress Teimei|Emperor Teimei]] was enshrined in [[1913|1913]], [[Taishō|Taisho]], [[Hirose-cho, Hiroshima Prefecture]], and [[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|Prince Chichibu]] and [[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu]] were established in [[1924|1924]] (Taisho 13) in [[Himeji|Himeji City]], Doshin-cho.
The birthplace of [[Emperor Hirohito|Showa]] was established in [[1923|1923]] (Taisho 12) in Yoshitsune, Honbetsu Village, [[Tokachi District, Hokkaido|Tokachi]], Hokkaido, and the birthplace of Emperor Showa and [[Empress Kōjun|Empress Kōjun]] was established in [[1928|1928]] (Showa 3) in Hirose-cho, Hiroshima City.

This is the one of [[Emperor Meiji]]
[[Emperor Meiji]] was enshrined at Ishinomaki Port, Miyagi Prefecture in [[1876|1876]] ([[Meiji (era)|明治]]9), and at Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village, [[Kamiina District, Nagano|Kamiina County]] in [[1893|1893]] Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village. Emperor Meiji and [[Empress Shōken|Emperor Shōken]] were enshrined in two places in [[1887|1887]] and [[1904|1904]], and [[Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]] was enshrined in [[1911|1911]] in [[1944]]. [[Emperor Taishō|Taisho]] was enshrined at two locations in [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka, Hokkaidō|Hidaka]], [[Kadobetsu]], [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka, Hokkaidō|Hidaka]], [[Empress Teimei|Emperor Teimei]] was enshrined in [[1913|1913]], [[Taishō|Taisho]], [[Hirose-cho, Hiroshima Prefecture]], and [[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|Prince Chichibu]] and [[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu]] were established in [[1924|1924]] (Taisho 13) in [[Himeji|Himeji City]], Doshin-cho.
The birthplace of [[Emperor Hirohito|Showa]] was established in [[1923|1923]] (Taisho 12) in Yoshitsune, Honbetsu Village, [[Tokachi District, Hokkaido|Tokachi]], Hokkaido, and the birthplace of Emperor Showa and [[Empress Kōjun|Empress Kōjun]] was established in [[1928|1928]] (Showa 3) in Hirose-cho, Hiroshima City.

{{AfC submission|t||ts=20220218132857|u=SiliconProphet|ns=118|demo=}}
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects to [[Sheng_Shrine]] before draftification: *[[Seishi (Shinto)]] --> A {{ill|Sheng Shrine|ja|生祀|zh|生祠}} is a [[shrine]] for a living person. There are two types of enshrinement: enshrining the spirit of a living person who has made a significant contribution and enshrining one's own spirit. [[File:Hiraga_Gennai_seishi01s2040.jpg|thumb|200x200px|[[Hiraga Gennai|Hiraga Gennai]]'s birth shrine in [[Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], [[Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama City]], Tomomachi.]]
== Overview ==
=== Sheng shrines in China === When [[Han Dynasty#Western Han|Former Han]]'s Reunobu was the prime minister of [[Yan Kingdom (Han dynasty)|Yan]], he built his shrine between Yan and [[Qi (state)|Qi]] and called it the "Reun Gong Shrine". Also, when [[:zh:石慶|Shiqing]] was the prime minister of Qi, the Qi people built the "Shiqing Shrine". This is the beginning of the birth shrine.<ref>[[Zhao Yi]], "The New New Ancestral Hall", Volume 32, "Ancestral Hall", "This is the beginning of the establishment of an ancestral hall.</ref>
In the [[Tang dynasty]], there were certain restrictions on the construction of monuments and shrines by officials in office. According to the [[Tang Code|Tang Rulings and Ordinances]], those who erected shrines or monuments without permission were subject to a penalty of one year of imprisonment.<ref>The [[Tang Code]], Volume 11 (The Law of Office)</ref>。The Tang Dynasty called it "the difficulty of building monuments and shrines on the first day.
In the [[Ming dynasty|Ming]], peasants in [[Tengzhou|Tengzhou]] built a shrine to commemorate [[:zh:趙邦清|Zhao Bangqing]], who was appointed to Yanjing for his clean politics. The shrine was built to commemorate him.
During the reign of the [[Tianqi Emperor]] of the Ming Dynasty, [[Wei Zhongxian]], the eunuch [[:zh:存禮監]] [[:zh:秉筆太監]], ruled the world by conducting his own politics, and those who flattered him or feared his authority were given a He made a living shrine for him. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing (1627), Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote: "Confucius wrote the Spring and Autumn, and Chung-Hsien wrote the Essentials. In May of the seventh year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, Lu Wanling, the prefect of the State Council, wrote that Confucius had written the "Spring and Autumn" and Chung-Hsien had written the "Essentials. In April of the 7th year of the reign of Emperor Tianjing, [[Yuan Chonghuan]] and [[:zh:閻鳴泰|Yan Mingtai]] of the [[Ministry of War (imperial China)|Shohshoh of the Ministry of War]] made an appeal to the government to praise the merits and demerits of Wei Zhongxian and to establish a living shrine for him in both Qiantu.<ref>『明熹宗実録』："天啓七年四月“薊遼總督閻鳴泰、巡撫袁崇煥疏，頌魏忠賢功德，請於寧前建祠，賜名懋德。」袁崇煥疏稱:「廠臣魏忠賢功在社稷，海內之共見共聞，業已銘刻金石，無容職贅，至其身任遼事，誓□恢復，梟滅逆虜，任用劉應乾、陶文、紀用等，而關內外禦敵之伏甲軍器馬匹懸簾等項，俱以家資置辦，日逐解來，又助軍需。臣方一意巡緝，嚴警諸營將吏，不敢貪懦營私，不敢餽遺隱串，改虛為實，化賈為真，易怯為勇，以有今日。浞古內臣誰有出其右者！"（『明熹宗七年都察院実録』天啓六年十月）<!--Is this Chinese? It is from Japanese Wikipedia so I am confused as to translation--></ref>。After that, Wei Zhongxian's raw shrine was "all over the world", "the cost of each shrine was at most hundreds of thousands, at least tens of thousands", "stolen the people's fortune, invaded the public safe, and was cut down. There are countless trees. " When Huang Yun Yasushi made a statue and greeted the statue, he said, "Goto Sansho," and "leaded the general of Bunbu, lined up under the stairs, and worshiped as the beginning."
[[Gu Yanwu|Gu Yanwu]] lamented, "In this generation, there is no official who does not build a living shrine. And within a few years of leaving office, he would destroy the statues and change their masters. He said.<ref>《Nichi Chi Roku》 vol. 23 (Sheng Shi), p. 644.</ref>。
=== Shosha in Japan === Shosha, the worship of one's own spirit, was performed in order to obtain a long life or to become a god after death. It is said to have originated from the legend of [[Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi-no-mikoto]], who enshrined his [[Mitama|Chikishin]] and [[Mitama|Kozoshin]] in [[Mount Miwa|Sanmorozan]] ([[Mount Miwa]]).
The earliest documented case of shosha enshrining one's own spirit is that of Haruhiko Matsuki ([[824|824]]), a [[Shinkan (official) priest]] of the outer shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[923|923]] of the [[Heian period]]. - [[924|924 years]]), enshrined and enshrined his own spirit on a stone in Obe, Ise [[Watarai District, Mie|Tokai County]]. In the [[Edo period]], [[Matsudaira Sadanobu|Matsudaira Sadanobu]] established his own nascent shrine in [[1797|1797]] at Shirakawa Castle in Oshu. The number of shosshiki increased during the Edo period, probably due to the influence of Chinese thought. In the [[Edo period]], [[Yamazaki Ansai|Yakusai Yamazaki]] devised a [[Festival]] to enshrine his own spirit, referring to the rituals of [[Confucianism]]. The Shusei ritual was established in 1671 at the Tarukarei Shrine in his residence in Kyoto. After this, the [[Shinto]] family and the [[Kokugaku]] scholars of the Hirata school enshrined their own spirits in their own unique rituals.
Kato Genchi ([[1873|1873]] - [[1965|1965]]) is famous for his research on Shusei. According to Kato, there are 670 known Shuso enshrines, and the newest one is that of Nishi Tadayoshi (father of Nishi Yoshikazu, who was enshrined in Urakawa-cho, Hidaka Province, Hokkaido in August [[1931|1931]]). This is the one of [[Emperor Meiji]]
[[Emperor Meiji]] was enshrined at Ishinomaki Port, Miyagi Prefecture in [[1876|1876]] ([[Meiji (era)|明治]]9), and at Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village, [[Kamiina District, Nagano|Kamiina County]] in [[1893|1893]] Yahiko Shrine in Ono Village. Emperor Meiji and [[Empress Shōken|Emperor Shōken]] were enshrined in two places in [[1887|1887]] and [[1904|1904]], and [[Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]] was enshrined in [[1911|1911]] in [[1944]]. [[Emperor Taishō|Taisho]] was enshrined at two locations in [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka, Hokkaidō|Hidaka]], [[Kadobetsu]], [[Hokkaido]], [[Hidaka, Hokkaidō|Hidaka]], [[Empress Teimei|Emperor Teimei]] was enshrined in [[1913|1913]], [[Taishō|Taisho]], [[Hirose-cho, Hiroshima Prefecture]], and [[Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|Prince Chichibu]] and [[Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu]] were established in [[1924|1924]] (Taisho 13) in [[Himeji|Himeji City]], Doshin-cho.
The birthplace of [[Emperor Hirohito|Showa]] was established in [[1923|1923]] (Taisho 12) in Yoshitsune, Honbetsu Village, [[Tokachi District, Hokkaido|Tokachi]], Hokkaido, and the birthplace of Emperor Showa and [[Empress Kōjun|Empress Kōjun]] was established in [[1928|1928]] (Showa 3) in Hirose-cho, Hiroshima City.
== References == <references />
== See Also ==
* [[Tamaya]] * {{ill|Okuto Castle|ja|奥都城}}

The government of the Liaoning Province has been inscribed in gold and stone, and there is no room for extra duties. The minister's single-mindedness in patrolling, strict warning of the battalion generals, do not dare to greed cowardly selfishness, do not dare to give away the hidden string, change the virtual into the real, into the real, easy timid into brave, to have today. Sustainability ancient internal minister who has the right!" ("Ming Xi Zong seven years of the Imperial Court of the Department of the Experiment" Tianqi six years in October)</ref>。After that, Wei Zhongxian's raw shrine was "all over the world", "the cost of each shrine was at most hundreds of thousands, at least tens of thousands", "stolen the people's fortune, invaded the public safe, and was cut down.
